topic: short-cycle grape varieties and cool-season suitability
trigger_keywords: afirmaciones sobre las variedades de ciclo corto
más adecuadas para regiones con estaciones de crecimiento cortas o frías

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A grape variety needs comparatively fewer heat and time resources to progress from budbreak to physiological and flavour maturity.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Its shorter phenological cycle allows ripening to finish within a shorter or cooler growing season before autumn temperatures fall or weather risks increase.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The variety is more likely to reach suitable maturity in cool or short-season regions than a later-ripening variety, although site and vintage conditions still matter.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: A short-cycle variety can complete ripening with fewer heat and time resources, making it better suited to short or cool growing seasons; this does not guarantee quality by itself.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
